questions.q: The process of arranging the business resources such that they are most effectively and efficiently utilised is known as _______.
Which of the following is a characteristic of an effective organisational structure?

questions.answer: Departmentalisation is the process of grouping activities that are closely related or similar in nature.

questions.q: What are the advantages of a functional organisational structure? Mention any two.
questions.answer: The advantages of a functional organisational structure are: (i) It promotes specialisation and expertise. (ii) It facilitates effective communication.

questions.q: Explain the concept of line and staff relationships.
questions.answer: Line and staff relationships refer to the relationships between line authorities and staff authorities within an organisation. Line authorities are responsible for implementing the decisions of the organisation, while staff authorities provide technical advice and support to the line authorities.

questions.q: Fill in the blanks: _______ is a type of departmentalisation where activities are grouped on the basis of products or services.
Compare and contrast the advantages and disadvantages of a functional and a divisional organisational structure.
questions.answer: Functional organisational structure: Advantages: promotes specialisation and expertise, facilitates effective communication. Disadvantages: may lead to lack of coordination, may not be suitable for large organisations. Divisional organisational structure: Advantages: suitable for large organisations, promotes decentralisation. Disadvantages: may lead to duplication of efforts, may not promote specialisation.

questions.q: State the importance of departmentalisation in an organisation.
questions.answer: Departmentalisation is essential in an organisation as it promotes specialisation, facilitates effective communication, and increases efficiency.

questions.q: Explain the concept of decentralisation of authority.
questions.answer: Decentralisation of authority refers to the delegation of authority from the top management to lower levels of management. This enables the organisation to make decisions quickly and respond to changing situations.

questions.q: Which of the following is a disadvantage of a functional organisational structure?
